text: With the development of large language models (LLMs), in-context learning (ICL) has become a new approach in natural language processing (NLP), where the LLM predicts the next word by seeing the input context with a few examples. ICL has been widely used to evaluate and forecast the potential of LLMs. This paper reviews the progresses and challenges of ICL. First, we define the ICL and link it to other relative work. Then, we introduce the advanced techniques in ICL, including training methods, prompt design strategies and so on. After that, we explore the application of ICL in data engineering and knowledge updating, and summarize the challenges and future research directions. With this paper, we hope to inspire more studies that aim at understanding ICL and improving ICL. 1.
